channel.title: Network on sramazoth
channel.description: Recent content in Network on sramazoth

item.title: Breaking ETIC Telecom IPL-DEC-220
item.description: informations Probably linked to this CVE : https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-3703 Which equipment : ETIC IPL-DEC-220 (website link) source : https://www.etictelecom.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/IPL-DEC-220-LE-272x490.png.webp Some IP addresses have been changed and screenshots may use french but sources are shown. recon TCP network scan : Network scan Webpage on 80 Home page on port 80 The page is basic, two links are present and redirect to : https://10.0.0.1//common.etic_cgi_portal/login.html https://10.0.0.1:4433/login.html Network scan didn’t find port 4433 because of my options which were very basic.

item.title: Setting and using scapy mitm
item.description: requirements two physical network interfaces : ip a 1: lo: [ . . . ] 2: eth0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state DOWN group default qlen 1000 link/ether 50:81:40:78:96:ac brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff 3: eth1: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state DOWN group default qlen 1000 link/ether 50:81:40:88:85:bd brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff scapy is installed and working (already done if using kali) two equipments talking to each other using a physical network (server and client on the sketch), I’m not doing wireless mitm here it might be useful to disable NetworkManager to avoid interfaces to go off : sudo systemctl disable NetworkManager sudo systemctl stop NetworkManager sketch Mitm setup
